text:
NBA



PARIS — After anNBAgame in Cleveland last weekend,San Antonio SpursrookieVictor Wembanyamasuggested he would accept an invitation to play for Team France in the upcoming 2024 Paris Olympics if he is asked.

Well, guess what? The invite is in the mail.

“This is (France coach) Vincent (Collet’s) call to tell us this, but yes, we’re sure,” Jean-Pierre Suitat, president of the French basketball federation, toldThe Athleticon Wednesday.

And what of Collet?

“We’ll ask him for sure,” Collet said.

Advertisement

It’s settled then. France, hosting the 2024 Olympics, with arguably the greatest international men’s basketball tournament field ever on the way, is likely going to have the 20-year-old Parisian sensation and No. 1 pick from the 2023 NBA Draft on its side.



Suitat and Collet sat separately withThe Athleticthis week for interviews at the French federation’s headquarters on the southwest side of Paris.Collet, the longtime French national team’s coach, also was Wembanyama’s coach last season for the Metropolitans 92 of the LNB Pro A — the top tier of France’s pro league. Wembanyama played for Collet on Team France in qualifiers for the 2023 FIBA World Cup but pulled himself off the roster before the World Cup itself.

“It was frustrating but necessary,” Wembanyama said last weekend of his decision to miss the World Cup, in which the French failed to advance past the first round. “I wanted to play — I want to play every time I can represent my country. But the thing is, the math is, I want to be there for the next 15 years, not just one summer. So we needed to be smart.”

Asked about theOlympicsimmediately after he explained his decision from last summer, Wembanyama said: “This is the big, big event of French basketball. And I said I want to play every time. So, yes, if I’m selected, if I’m available, I want to play.”

Wembanyamanotched his first NBA triple-doublein the Spurs’ win Wednesday against theDetroit Pistons. Entering the day, he led the NBA in blocks at 3.3 per game and was the top rookie scorer in the league at 19.3 points per. He averaged 19 points and 8.3 boards in four games with Collet playing for France in FIBA qualifiers and 21.6 points and 3.0 blocks for Collet on the Metropolitans.

Before the Spurs drafted him No. 1 last summer, Wembanyama had said publicly he would play in the World Cup, and when he changed his mind after joining the Spurs for the NBA Summer League in Las Vegas, it appeared Spurs coach (and former Team USA coach) Gregg Popovich had talked him off the French roster. But Suitat said that wasn’t the case.

Advertisement

“We have a good, good relationship with them, because of Tony (Parker), because of Boris (Diaw),” Suitat said. “(Victor) wanted to play with the national team before the draft. He talked with Vincent, said, ‘Yeah, coach, don’t worry about me, I’ll be on the way.’ But he’s a young guy, and he did not realize really what happened during the draft and after the draft. And for him to be sure to be ready for his first (NBA season), we understand why (he didn’t play at the World Cup. We understand this; this is why we don’t pressure the players. What we want is him to be there later.

“With the San Antonio Spurs, we have this relationship of, we trust each other.”

To that end, Collet said, he and members of his coaching staff, as well as French basketball officials above him (like Diaw, who is the French general manager) would make a trip to the U.S. later this month to visit with several players expected to play for France this summer. When Collet goes to San Antonio, he says he’ll ask Popovich if he and his staff can stay for a few days and “understand the organization because we want to use some of the things to bring to the national team.”

“We want to see, to learn and perhaps to use things we could discover from NBA teams,” Collet said.

Collet lost to Popovich’s USA team in the Tokyo Olympics gold-medal game in 2021, after beating the Americans in the first game of that tournament, as well as in the 2019 FIBA World Cup.

Who knows what current USA coach Steve Kerr, a protege of Popovich, might think of Collet’s January tutorial? Then again, Collet has already hired Kenny Atkinson, one of Kerr’s top assistants on hisGolden State Warriorsstaff, to assist him on Team France this summer.

What is clear is, barring injury, Wembanyama and Collet will reunite at the Olympics, with stars from all over the world in France to play for a gold medal that’s been won by the Americans in the last four Olympic tournaments.

(Photo: Christian Petersen / Getty Images)

Joe Vardonis a senior NBA writer for The Athletic, based in Cleveland.Follow Joe on Twitter@joevardon
title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama expected to join Team France for 2024 Summer Olympics - The Athletic
author: Joe Vardon
description: Entering Wednesday's games, Wembanyama led the NBA in blocks at 3.3 per game and is the top rookie scorer at 19.3 points per contest.
keywords: San Antonio Spurs, NBA, Olympics, Top Sports News
